# Obangame Express 2026 maritime exercise returns to Cameroon for piracy drills

## Overview
The Obangame Express 2026 multinational maritime exercise returned to Cameroon, focusing on countering piracy, illegal fishing, and smuggling threats. Activities simulated real-world scenarios to enhance regional maritime security cooperation.

## Details
The exercise involved multiple nations practicing responses to Gulf of Guinea challenges like armed attacks and trafficking. It addressed unique operational difficulties in the region, including piracy incidents. Participating forces conducted joint drills to improve interoperability. No specific vessel names or casualties reported as it represented a training event. The exercise built on prior iterations emphasizing proportionate responses to maritime threats.[2][4]

**Location**: Cameroon, Gulf of Guinea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @MDAT_GoG, which covers Gulf of Guinea piracy maritime security armed attacks. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Gulf of Guinea piracy maritime security armed attacks will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
